nature_of_business.value: General contracting for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania

uw_questions.question: Any past bankruptcies, receivership, or reorganization?
Any losses or claims in the past 5 years?
Any pending litigation or regulatory actions?
Are any regulated chemicals (EPA RCRA, TSCA, OSHA HCS) stored, used, or disposed of on premises?
Any work involving asbestos, lead, or mold remediation?
Are metal fabrication or welding operations performed on premises?
Does applicant lease employees to or from other firms?
Does the applicant prepare, handle, or serve food for human consumption?
Any recall of products in the past 5 years?
Does the applicant operate as a franchisor or sell franchise rights to franchisees?
Any daycare or childcare operations on premises?
Is applicant involved in any joint ventures?
Does applicant own or operate any unmanned aircraft systems (UAS/drones)?

all_fields.nature_of_business: General contracting for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania

uw_questions.question: Does the applicant provide professional services or advice?
Are any premises/operations rented or leased to others?
Does the applicant have any work performed by subcontractors?
Are there any completed operations exposures?
Does the applicant perform any demolition work?
Are there any fireworks, explosives, or blasting operations?
Are products manufactured, sold, or distributed?
Does the applicant have any daycare or childcare operations?
Is there any work performed on or near bodies of water?
Are there swimming pools, hot tubs, or diving boards on premises?

raw_ground_truth.loss_run_label: Consolidated Loss Summary (prepared by Steel City Insurance Group)

description.value: Employee experienced a frozen shoulder to the left shoulder while—reaching overhead to install sprinkler heads. The worker was placed on light duty for 6 weeks and referred to an orthopedic specialist.
Employee sustained a fracture of the greater trochanter to the right hip while landing on the hip after–––––––––––––––––––––––––– falling from scaffolding.
Employee sustained a severe sprain to the right knee while climbing a 14-foot ladder while carrying tools. The injury required a splint and modified work duties for 4 weeks.

denial_reason.value: Pre-existing condition unrelated to work activity

description.value: Employee suffered a labral tear to the right hip while landing on the hip after falling from scaffolding. Occupational therapy was recommended for a period of 12 weeks.
Employee sustained a contusion and deep bruising to the right hip while—landing on the hip after falling from scaffolding. The employee was prescribed rest, ice, and a follow-up with a specialist.
Employee sustained a’hip flexor strain to the left hip while slipping on a’rain-soaked sidewalk outside the facility. MRI confirmed the injury and the employee underwent arthroscopic surgery.

Employee suffered a tibial plateau fracture to the​​​​​​​​​​​​​ left knee while stepping into a pothole in the parking lot. Orthopedic evaluation and casting were performed, followed by 8 weeks of physical therapy.
Employee suffered a sternum…

Employee sustained a dislocated shoulder to the right shoulder while—reaching overhead to install ductwork. Emergency reduction and immobilization were performed, followed by physical therapy for range of motion recovery.
Employee suffered a crush injury to the right foot while—stepping on a protruding nail at the construction site. Emergency medical treatment was administered on-site before transport to the hospital.
Employee sustained a SLAP tear...
Employee suffered a’meniscus tear to the right knee while climbing a’8-foot ladder while carrying tools. The worker was placed on light duty for 6 weeks and referred to an orthopedic specialist.
Employee suffered a fracture of…

Employee sustained a rib fracture to the​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ chest while falling against a steel railing during a slip. Surgery was required to repair the damage, followed by 8 weeks of rehabilitation.
Employee’s suffered a compression fracture to the​​​​​​​​​​​​​ lower back while shoveling debris at the job site. Treatment included physical therapy and a course of anti-inflammatory medication.
Employee sustained a...
Employee sustained multiple rib contusions to the​​​​​​​​​​​​​ chest while falling against a steel railing during a slip. The injury required a splint and modified work duties for 4 weeks.

Employee sustained a severe sprain to the right knee while climbing a 14-foot ladder while carrying tools. The injury required a splint and modified work duties for 4 weeks.
Employee suffered a labral tear to the right hip while landing on the hip after falling from scaffolding. Occupational therapy was recommended for a period of 12 weeks.
Employee sustained a contusion and deep bruising to the right hip while—landing on the hip after falling from scaffolding. The employee was prescribed rest, ice, and a follow-up with a specialist.
Employee sustained a’hip flexor strain to the left hip while slipping on a’rain-soaked sidewalk outside the facility. MRI confirmed the injury and the employee underwent arthroscopic surgery.

Employee suffered a tibial plateau fracture to the​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​​ left knee while stepping into a pothole in the parking lot. Orthopedic evaluation and casting were performed, followed by 8 weeks of physical therapy.

Employee suffered a crush injury to the right foot while—stepping on a protruding nail at the construction site. Emergency medical treatment was administered on-site before transport to the hospital.

table_of_contents.title: PART FOUR — YOUR DUTIES IF INJURY OCCURS

nature_of_business.value: General contracting for commercial and institutional construction — healthcare facilities, educational buildings, and corporate offices throughout Western Pennsylvania

risk_management.question: Are hazardous materials used or encountered in operations?
Are certificates of insurance required from all subcontractors before work begins?
What percentage of work is performed by subcontractors vs. own employees?
Is there a formal return-to-work or modified duty program?
Does the applicant maintain OSHA 300 logs and conduct regular job-site safety audits?
Does the applicant have a formal written safety program?
Are hold-harmless agreements obtained from all subcontractors?
Does the applicant perform any demolition or blasting operations?
Describe the three largest projects completed in the past 3 years.
risk_management.answer: 1) $1.2M restaurant build-out, 2) $800K medical office renovation, 3) $650K church addition

risk_management.question: Are any operations performed above 4 stories?
